title: Today's AI Brief: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
summary: A look at Endava's AI-powered software delivery and Yahoo's new AI tools for NBA fans

body.text: I am looking at some interesting developments in the world of AI. It seems like we're seeing a shift towards more practical applications of AI in various industries. Today, I'll be covering two news items that caught my attention: Endava's efforts to redesign software delivery around AI agents and Yahoo's new AI tools for NBA fans and investors.

Endava is using AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across the enterprise. What stands out to me here is how Endava is leveraging AI to streamline their software delivery process. By using AI agents, they're able to automate certain tasks and free up their teams to focus on more complex and creative work. It's an interesting example of how AI can be used to augment human capabilities, rather than replace them.
Yahoo's AI Tools for NBA Fans

Yahoo has launched new AI tools for NBA fans and investors. According to Axios, these tools aim to provide users with more personalized and up-to-date information about the NBA. I think it's interesting to see how Yahoo is using AI to enhance the fan experience. By providing more tailored content and insights, they're able to engage their users in a more meaningful way. It's a clever move, especially considering the competitive world of sports media.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
